title: Trace Analytics Value Processing
entry_points: Integration::setAnalyticsIfEnabled($span, $name) — integration auto-sets rate from env config
activity_flow: DECISION: is span finished AND setIfFinished==false? → [yes] STOP (no-op); [no] continue
DECISION: is key numeric / not a string? → [yes] STOP (guard); [no] continue
DECISION: is $key in $specialTags? → [yes] route to metric handler; [no] store in meta[]
DECISION: is $key in $metricNames (ANALYTICS_KEY is in $metricNames)? → [yes] call setMetric(key, value); [no] store in meta[]
BRANCH 4 — none of the above (e.g. negative, >1, non-numeric) → no-op (value silently ignored)
END: $internalSpan->metrics array updated (or unchanged for invalid values)
integration_auto_path: DECISION: integration_analytics_enabled($name) OR (NOT requiresExplicitTraceAnalyticsEnabling() AND DD_TRACE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED env==true)
key_decisions: false → remove key (opt-out / disable analytics for this span)
float in [0,1] → use as explicit sample rate
out-of-range or non-numeric → silently ignored, no state change
key_relationships: Span uses Tag::ANALYTICS_KEY constant as routing key
Integration writes directly to $span->metrics without normalizeAnalyticsValue
TraceAnalyticsProcessor is final class with no instance state (pure utility)
business_rules: Analytics key '_dd1.sr.eausr' is a Datadog-internal metric controlling trace analytics sample rate
Valid rate range is [0.0, 1.0] — outside values are silently dropped (no exception)
Boolean true/false are first-class values (not treated as 1/0 numeric)
false explicitly removes the metric (opt-out semantics, not just zero-rate)
Integration auto-instrumentation can set rate from DD_TRACE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED env var or per-integration config
ANALYTICS_KEY is in both $specialTags and $metricNames, ensuring setTag routes through setMetric
